title: pinguicula grandiflora var. longifolia
intro: Pinguicula grandiflora var. longifolia, also known as the long-flowered butterwort, is a carnivorous plant species belonging to the genus Pinguicula, characterized by its unique ability to capture and digest insects for essential nutrients. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine, and its leaves and roots are believed to possess medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, such as aiding digestion, reducing fever, and treating respiratory issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Herbal preparations of Pinguicula grandiflora var. longifolia are often made by infusing the leaves and roots in water or oil, which can be used as a decoction, infusion, or topical treatment, but caution is advised due to the plant's toxicity.
